response.text:
{
  "consensus": [
    "Plan-only pure validation: no workspace access, no loader execution, no network/env expansion, no live credential calls.",
    "Accept only strict well-formed JSON (RFC-style); invalid JSON fails immediately.",
    "Enforce exact frontier model IDs from supplied context: active direct seats must be exactly grok-4.5; openai/gpt-5.6-sol is at most optional/disabled.",
    "Closed schema with additionalProperties false at all depths; any unknown field fails.",
    "Reject plaintext secrets (pattern/structural rules); env-var references are allowed and are not secrets.",
    "Preserve environment-variable key names verbatim (no rewrite, case change, expansion, or normalization).",
    "Emit unambiguous deterministic PASS/FAIL (exit 0 vs non-zero) with stable error detail; identical input → identical output.",
    "Ship/release only on PASS; use golden pass/fail vectors as plan-level verification; treat MCP/166-tests/validation_errors=[] as static background evidence only."
  ],
  "contradictions": [
    "Optional GPT seat policy: A/B allow openai/gpt-5.6-sol when explicitly disabled/absent-credential; C defaults to reject-if-present while no usable routed credential and ties presence to a credential-routable flag.",
    "Result contract shape: A prefers terminal PASS or FAIL plus sorted rule-ID list; B prefers structured {status, validation_errors, ...} mirroring validation_errors=[]; C prefers richer object including checked_models and sha256 config_fingerprint.",
    "Whether validation_errors==[] is an intrinsic pass criterion of this check (B lists it explicitly) versus only historical evidence used to justify allow-lists (A/C).",
    "Secret-rule concreteness: C bakes specific regexes/prefixes into the plan; A/B keep denylist abstract and implementation-pinned—risk of false confidence vs under-specification.",
    "Fail semantics: A emphasizes ordered fail-fast; B/C allow multi-error structured lists (C also discusses short-circuit vs multi-error extension)."
  ],
  "partial_coverage": [
    "Concrete loader field allow-list / full JSON Schema is acknowledged missing by all; plans defer to a frozen closed schema without supplying one.",
    "Exact env-reference syntax (${NAME} vs env:NAME vs structured object) is unknown; all require preservation regardless but cannot pin loader-native form.",
    "Disabled-marker canonical spelling for the optional GPT seat is unspecified across seats.",
    "Depth/size bounds and duplicate-key handling are mentioned as edges but not fully standardized.",
    "How preservation is asserted (round-trip equality on reference strings vs keys-only) is described at different levels of rigor."
  ],
  "unique_insights": [
    "A: FAIL messaging as stable sorted violated rule IDs; audit transcript of preserved env names; explicit meta-step forbidding plan-time tool/filesystem claims.",
    "B: Implementer checklist artifact; result fields for preserved_env_keys and models_accepted; minority option for schema-less hermetic walker vs JSON Schema.",
    "C: Concrete secret regex set; canonical-JSON sha256 fingerprint for determinism; ~100 LOC auditability target; unicode/zero-width adversarial cases; independent second implementation diff on fixtures.",
    "C: Explicit adversarial hidden-assumption list (non-JSON inputs, non-string secrets, indirection activating non-frontier models, prompt-injection in values mitigated by non-execution)."
  ],
  "minority_findings": [
    "Stricter GPT posture: reject openai/gpt-5.6-sol outright in any release config until usable routed credential exists (A minority; echoed in B/C variants)—supported by context’s disabled/no-credential statement and reduces latent enablement; preserve as optional hardening.",
    "B: Allow plaintext only under explicit non-release/dev profile flag—conflicts with task’s unconditional plaintext rejection for this release-check; keep rejection unconditional for the gate.",
    "B: Zero-dependency hand-rolled structural walker for hermeticity instead of JSON Schema—valid if schema tooling is unavailable; primary clarity still favors frozen schema.",
    "C: Cryptographically sign allow-list/schema; treat secret scanning as externally versioned ruleset—useful supply-chain hardening, not required by task wording.",
    "C: Do not trust pre-existing validation_errors=[] alone as proof—correct adversarial stance; use as complementary signal only."
  ],
  "blind_spots": [
    "No seat possesses the real loader contract/example configs; allow-lists and required keys remain inferred—plan must mark schema as an externally supplied frozen artifact.",
    "Mechanical evidence (MCP OK, 13 tools, 166 tests, empty validation_errors) does not prove secret/unknown-field paths are exercised; risk of correlated over-trust in baseline health.",
    "Hard-coding today’s model IDs without an explicit amendment process if providers change (A notes version skew; still under-developed as an ops control).",
    "Credential-routable language (C) can blur pure plan-only validation if misread as runtime credential checks—must stay config-static flags only.",
    "Secret pattern completeness: all admit false negatives/positives; none define entropy thresholds or update ownership.",
    "Nested arrays-of-models, non-string secret carriers, and loader acceptance of non-JSON formats are largely untested assumptions.",
    "DoS/resource bounds for large/deep JSON mentioned lightly; not a first-class deterministic gate requirement in all plans."
  ],
  "final_guidance": [
    "Author a single concise ordered pure-function plan: (1) strict JSON parse, (2) frozen closed schema additionalProperties=false, (3) exact ID const/enum grok-4.5 required for active seats, (4) plaintext-secret reject via documented pinned patterns, (5) env-ref key-name verbatim preservation, (6) unambiguous PASS/FAIL + stable errors + exit codes.",
    "Pin model policy to evidence: require grok-4.5; permit openai/gpt-5.6-sol only if schema-enforced disabled/absent-credential marker; surface the stricter reject-if-present stance as an optional tighter gate, not the default claim.",
    "Do not invent full field lists or env syntax—state they are frozen external artifacts derived from loader contract; cite mechanical evidence only as static justification, never runtime proof.",
    "Prefer a structured machine result (status pass|fail, error codes list, preserved env keys) while still allowing a one-line PASS/FAIL summary; require determinism (no time/RNG/network/env reads) and golden vectors covering each task requirement.",
    "Keep secret rejection unconditional for release-check; keep plan-only language explicit; include edge cases (unknown nested fields, ID variants, plaintext vs ${ENV}, missing seats, duplicate keys, size/depth bounds) without claiming execution.",
    "Preserve lone-correct hardenings worth merging: sorted stable rule IDs, canonical fingerprint optional, explicit amendment path when model IDs change, and distrust of validation_errors=[] as sole signal."
  ]
}
